name: Paella (Deck)
recipeInstructions:
1. If you don't have the traditional paella pan, a shallow, heavy, large skillet can be used.
2. Wash and dry the chicken. Sauté in hot olive oil for a few minutes, then remove to a pot with 4 cups of water. Boil the chicken for 15 minutes.
3. Meanwhile, fry onions and bell peppers in the same skillet. Add diced tomato.
4. Rinse seafood and add it to the skillet with onions, bell peppers, and tomato. Cut pimentos into strips and add half to the skillet along with the peas.
5. Mash garlic, parsley, and salt together with the back of a wooden spoon and add to the pan.
6. Add saffron, then chicken, rice, and sausage. Cover with 4 cups boiling chicken broth.
7. Stir gently to distribute ingredients evenly. Bring to a boil for five minutes; then reduce heat to medium and continue cooking until rice is tender and dry and grains are separated, about 20 to 30 minutes. Do not stir during cooking.
8. When done, adjust seasoning with red pepper if desired. Garnish with remaining pimento strips and lemon wedges. Serve from the pan. Serves six.
